question_text: Let X1, X2, . . . , X18 be a random sample from the distribution f(x; θ) = θ e−x2/θ, x > 0, x ≤0. What is the maximum likelihood estimator (MLE) of θ?

Quick Tip
The maximum likelihood estimator (MLE) for a parameter is found by differentiating
the log-likelihood function and setting it equal to zero. In cases where the likelihood
involves sums of squares, the MLE is typically the sample mean or a scaled version of
it.
